security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote message could be altered without detection.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system lacks tamper-evident mechanisms for votes.

assumptions: The governance contract does not validate the integrity of vote messages.
The actor can intercept and modify the vote message before it reaches the contract.

mitigation.strategy: Implement tamper-evident mechanisms, such as data hashing and digital signatures.

description: An attacker may alter a legitimate vote to change its outcome.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes are not immutably linked to the voter's identity.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Vote submission does not guarantee non-repudiation.

assumptions: The system does not enforce a mechanism to definitively prove who cast a specific vote.
The actor is capable of casting a vote anonymously.

mitigation.strategy: Implement mechanisms for verifiable and auditable votes, such as blockchain-based record keeping.

description: An attacker may cast votes and subsequently deny having done so.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be exposed to unauthorized actors.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Vote data is potentially vulnerable to unauthorized access.

assumptions: The governance contract does not adequately protect vote data from unauthorized disclosure.
The actor is able to access the system's database or network traffic.

mitigation.strategy: Implement access controls and encryption to protect vote data.

description: An attacker may gain access to sensitive voting information.
